underlying_goal: Rebuild a TCP listener for SIA DC09 alarm reception from scratch, understanding every piece intimately rather than using AI-generated code
friction_detail: Claude initially suggested a context+watcher goroutine approach for graceful shutdown that was overcomplicated and leaky; user pushed back and they pivoted to a simpler Option B approach.
brief_summary: User wanted to rebuild a TCP server for SIA alarm reception step-by-step to deeply understand it, and they made substantial progress through 7 steps (basic server, read loop, goroutines, SIA framing, timeouts, connection limits, graceful shutdown) with config/logging integration still pending.
